joke:
Why don't skeletons fight each other?  

They don’t have the guts.

Why don't scientists trust atoms?  

Because they make up everything!

Why did the coffee file a police report?  

It got mugged every morning!

Why did the bicycle fall over?  

It was two-tired!

Why did the tomato turn red?  

Because it saw the salad dressing!

Why did the math book look so sad?  

It had too many problems.

Why did the calendar go to therapy?  

It had too many dates to deal with!

Why did the belt get arrested?  

It was holding up a pair of pants!

Why did the painter quit his job?  

He just couldn't draw a paycheck!

Why did the tree get promoted?  

It had deep roots in management!

Why did the cloud break up with the mountain?  

It needed space!

Why did the invisible man turn down the job offer?  

He couldn’t see himself doing it!

Why did the scarecrow win an award?  

Because he was outstanding in his field!

Why did the belt get kicked out of the movie?  

It kept holding up the plot!

Why did the skeleton refuse to perform stand-up comedy?

Because no matter what I do, you’re never going to laugh at *anything*!

Why did the comedian quit?  

He just couldn’t get a rise out of anyone—except maybe a tombstone, but we don’t talk about that.

Why did the abstract concept of irony refuse to explain itself?  

Because the moment I say it, it’s no longer ironic—it’s just sad.

Why did the comedian bring a ladder to the bar?  

Because he heard the drinks were on the house… but honestly, at this point, I’m just trying to climb out of this conversation.

Why did the comedian start talking to himself?  

Because even his echo stopped answering!

Why did the silence break up with the pause?  

Because it just couldn’t handle the tension… much like this audience.

Why did the comedian stop telling jokes?  

Because apparently, the only thing left to joke about is this never-ending list of things you hate—and I’m not touching that with a ten-foot pole… or a microphone.

Why did the comedian bring a mirror to the stage?

Because at this point, the only person laughing should be me—looking at you.

Why did the comedian go to therapy?  

Because every time he tried to tell a joke, someone filed a complaint!

Why did the comedian show up to the gig with a flashlight?  

Because clearly, no one here brings their own light!

Why did the comedian start carrying a pencil?  

Because apparently, no one here likes punchlines—so he figured he’d just draw something you *can’t* complain about.

Why did the comedian sit down?

Because standing up clearly isn’t helping!

Why did the comedian bring a map to the stage?

Because he’s officially lost in the wilderness of your disapproval!

Why did the comedian start whispering?

Because even silence deserves a standing ovation after this!

Why did the comedian stop talking?  

Because even his shadow walked away to get some peace and quiet!

Why did the comedian bring a mirror to the stage?

Because apparently, the only person who finds me funny is me… and even I’m starting to question that.
